title: Customize cover corpus to match user context
description: Edit data/cover-corpus.json. Current theme is nerdy-14-year-old-violinist. Replace with work-flavored prompts if a boss is more likely to inspect (SQL, emails, code reviews).

title: Onboard the 5 recipients to the bot
description: Tell each recipient the bot username and their slot name (alex, sam, jordan, taylor, morgan). Have each send /start <slot> once. Verify via GET /admin/joins.

title: Create Telegram bot via @BotFather
description: Chat with @BotFather, run /newbot, pick a boring name. Copy the bot token; needed as TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN Worker secret.

title: Polish: rename recipient slots to actual coworker names
description: Update DEFAULT_MODELS_REAL in scripts/app.js with real coworker handles for the model -> recipient_id mapping. The frontend label can stay 'GPT-4o' etc.; only the recipient_id matters.

title: Switch api.js out of mock mode
description: Edit scripts/api.js: set WORKER_URL, SHARED_SECRET, flip USE_MOCK to false. Re-deploy to GitHub Pages. Blocked on Worker being live.

title: Push to private GitHub repo and enable Pages
description: git init + create a private GitHub repo with non-descriptive name (e.g. 'notes' or 'tools'). Enable Pages from main / root. Note the URL.

title: Register Worker as Telegram webhook
description: After Worker deploys, POST to /admin/setwebhook with the Worker URL. One-shot, documented in README.

title: Fine-tune hotspot position after first real test
description: Once running on the actual demo machine, verify the hotspot is reachable but not blocking interactive UI. Edit .hotspot rule in styles/main.css. Uncomment DEBUG rule for visual feedback during tuning.

title: Deploy Cloudflare Worker for Telegram bridge
description: Run wrangler login + KV namespace create + secrets setup + deploy. Steps in README.md section 'Setting up the Telegram backend'. Blocked on user creating the Telegram bot via @BotFather first.
